titleStmt.title: The Consul General at Shanghai (Cunningham) to the Secretary of State

head.hi: The Consul General at Shanghai (Cunningham) to the Secretary of State

div.p: Continuing my January 31, 4 p.m., the Committee of Defense stated that the proposal contained in my telegram was not feasible and that they could not execute it. The committee stated that the only feasible plan about [would be?] a complete withdrawal of the Japanese from the [tongue?] into the International Settlement and the placing of the tongue under the control of a neutral power and the Chinese moving their line 2,000 yards to the west of the railway. This was accepted for the Chinese but declined [by?] the Japanese who refused to [have their forces?] removed from the tongue. The meeting was a disappointment because of the very strong attitude of the Japanese against continuing the truce without the withdrawing of the Chinese military line but [this?] was eventually accepted, therefore, the truce continues until further notice. The Japanese Consul General agreed to submit the proposal of the Defense Committee to his Government for consideration, and upon the receipt of a reply if unfavorable the Chinese would then submit matter to their Government. I have very little confidence in the truce. The proposal of the committee would be an ideal solution but it is certain that neither the Japanese Admiral nor the Consul General will endorse the proposal.
